title: COUNSELING PROGRAM - 211info
text: WEST EUGENE COUNSELING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers outpatient mental health treatment. Therapists can address a range of mental, emotional, behavioral, and situational issues, including family conflict, physical and sexual abuse, trauma, grief, adjustment problems, co-occurring disorders, ADD/ADHD, school problems, domestic violence, sexual acting out, socialization problems, self-destructive behaviors, depression, anxiety/stress, anger management, self-esteem issues, relationship problems, and emotional problems. 260 E 11th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Unrestricted; varies by program Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm, Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 484-4428 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Intake (541) 484-4428 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: Varies by service DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Lane County Services: Adolescent/Youth Counseling Child Abuse Counseling Child Sexual Abuse Counseling Crime Victim/Witness Counseling Family Counseling Individual Counseling Sexual Assault Counseling Spouse/Intimate Partner Abuse Counseling Other Services Offered At This Location: FOOD - RESOURCE PANTRY SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - ADOLESCENT RECOVERY PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. WEST EUGENE
